name: Abkhaz

sources.author: Hewitt, B. George
sources.title: Abkhaz (Lingua Descriptive Studies)
sources.notes: Reference grammar; the source to use when filling the inventory.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode abkh1244.

notes: Abkhaz (аԥсшәа) — Northwest Caucasian language of Abkhazia, written in an extended Cyrillic alphabet since 1954. THE GRAPHEME INVENTORY IS NOT YET SPECIFIED in this spec (graphemes/allophones are empty). The alphabet has 62+ letters and digraphs encoding a very large consonant inventory (~58 consonants, only 2 phonemic vowels /a ə/), with three-way plain/labialised/palatalised series and productive ejectives; a reliable letter→IPA table needs research against a reference grammar (Hewitt 1979) before it can be claimed here. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode abkh1244), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

ancestors.notes: Abkhaz-Adyge — the classification node this language sits under.
